Energy-based Constrained Decoding with Langevin Dynamics (COLD) uniﬁes constrained generation by specifying constraints through an energy function and performing efficient differentiable reasoning over the constraints through gradient-based sampling

Energy-based Constrained Decoding with Langevin Dynamics (COLD) is a flexible framework that can be applied directly to off-the-shelf left-to-right language models without the need for any task-specific fine-tuning

Sampling text from an EBM using Langevin dynamics requires bridging the gap with continuous relaxation of text, differentiable constraints, and guided discretization.
COLD decoding treats text generation as sampling from an energy-based distribution, enabling flexible composition of constraints based on the task at hand.
COLD decoding generates text by sampling from an EBM defined over a sequence of 'soft' tokens using Langevin dynamics, then maps the continuous sample into discrete, fluent text.

We study a specific formulation of abductive reasoning as a language generation challenge
The abductive language generation problem involves generating a bridge sentence that fills in between the beginning and ending sentences to form a coherent full story.

The abductive language generation problem is challenging for conventional monotonic left-to-right LMs like GPT-2 and GPT-3 as it requires non-monotonic reasoning involving past context (xl) and future story ending (xr).

The COLD Solution can accommodate the abductive reasoning task by using appropriate constraints to specify an energy function for generating text that is fluent and consistent with the left context (xl) and coherent with the right context (xr).

COLD decoding can readily accommodate the abductive reasoning task by plugging in constraints to specify an energy function for generating text that meets certain conditions related to the left and right contexts.

We report the average runtime of generating one sample on the Counterfactual Story Rewriting data.

COLD, which uses gradient-based sampling, is faster than the gradient-free Mix-and-Match: COLD is 30% faster with base LMs of similar sizes (GPT2-M and BERTLarge), and has roughly the same time cost when using a much larger LM (GPT2-XL).
